title: 10 possible side effects of Picrasma excelsa  (sumatra)

side_effects_list.side_effect_desc: Picrasma excelsa causes allergic reactions in some individuals. This is due to the plant's chemical composition, which includes alkaloids and phenolic compounds that can trigger an immune response in sensitive people. These chemicals can bind to immune cells, leading to the release of histamine and other signaling molecules, causing symptoms such as hives, itching, and swelling.

Picrasma excelsa, also known as Sumatra, triggers skin rashes in some individuals. This is due to its ability to cause an allergic reaction, resulting in redness, itching, and inflammation of the skin. The tree's bark and leaves contain a compound called picrinic acid, which can trigger this response in people with sensitive skin.

Picrasma excelsa, also known as Sumatra, induces dizziness in some individuals due to its ability to interact with certain neurotransmitters and alter brain activity. The active compounds present in the plant, such as picrahydine and picroside, can affect the body's balance and equilibrium, leading to feelings of lightheadedness or disorientation. This side effect is thought to be more common when taking high doses or using Picrasma excelsa for extended periods.

Picrasma excelsa produces respiratory issues due to its chemical composition, which includes compounds that can irritate the lungs and airways. The alkaloids present in the plant can cause bronchospasm, inflammation, and increased mucus production, leading to symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. Additionally, the plant's volatile oils may exacerbate existing respiratory conditions, such as asthma or chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).

Picrasma excelsa increases blood pressure due to its ability to constrict blood vessels and stimulate the cardiovascular system. The bioactive compounds present in the herb, such as quinones and terpenoids, can cause a significant increase in systolic and diastolic blood pressure, which may lead to hypertension or exacerbate existing conditions. This side effect is thought to be related to the herb's ability to mimic the effects of adrenaline, causing increased heart rate and cardiac output.

Picrasma excelsa aggravates anxiety due to its stimulating properties that can increase heart rate and blood pressure. The herb's alkaloid content may overstimulate the nervous system, leading to feelings of restlessness, agitation, and heightened sense of alertness, which can exacerbate pre-existing anxiety conditions. This increased stimulation can also disrupt normal sleep patterns, further contributing to anxiousness and stress.

Picrasma excelsa disrupts digestive system by interfering with the normal functioning of the gut microbiome. The compound present in Picrasma excelsa can alter the balance of beneficial and harmful bacteria in the digestive tract, leading to symptoms such as bloating, abdominal pain, and changes in bowel movements. This disruption can also lead to malabsorption of nutrients, exacerbating underlying health conditions.

Picrasma excelsa leads to insomnia due to its ability to increase the levels of certain neurotransmitters in the brain, such as serotonin and norepinephrine. These chemicals can affect sleep patterns, leading to difficulty falling asleep or staying asleep. The herb's stimulating properties can also cause restlessness and anxiety, making it challenging for individuals to relax and fall into a peaceful slumber.

Picrasma excelsa causes headaches due to its ability to stimulate the body's natural detoxification processes. As it increases the production of enzymes that break down and remove toxins from the body, it can lead to a temporary increase in headaches. This is often a result of the body releasing stored toxins into the bloodstream, causing discomfort and pain in some individuals.

Picrasma excelsa affects liver function by disrupting its ability to process and eliminate toxins from the body. This is due to the presence of compounds like picroliv, which can inhibit certain enzymes involved in liver detoxification. As a result, users may experience altered liver function tests or develop signs of liver damage, such as jaundice or elevated liver enzymes.

intro: Picrasma excelsa has some side effects when used improperly, such as diarrhea, nausea, and vomiting. These symptoms can occur due to its high content of saponins, which can irritate the gastrointestinal tract and cause stomach upset. For instance, a person who experiences severe diarrhea may have to miss work or school, leading to financial losses and social isolation. In addition, chronic use can lead to malabsorption of nutrients, exacerbating underlying health conditions such as anemia or osteoporosis.
